commit.message:
Add host_wake_all_slots to pre-warm shadow FX before Song Mode playback

With Link Audio, Move's native track audio routes through shadow slots
for FX processing. When a track goes quiet, the slot idles and skips
rendering. On step transitions, the sudden audio arrival caused glitches
because FX state was stale. Wake all slots at playback start and each
step advance so FX chains run on zeros before audio arrives.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: Remove keyword-based taxonomy inference (v0.16.1)

Simplifies SDK to always use semantic matching, eliminating confusion
about keyword vs semantic matching strategies.

Changes:
- Removed inferTaxonomy() method (67 lines of keyword matching logic)
- Updated decideFromContext() to default to 'general.query' taxonomy
- Updated requestOfferFromContext() to use 'general.query' default
- Updated JSDoc to clarify semantic matching uses context, not taxonomy

Impact:
- Backward compatible - suggestedCategory parameter still works
- Semantic matching unaffected - backend ignores taxonomy for semantic campaigns
- Clearer developer experience - no hidden keyword inference
- Better performance - removed regex-heavy inference code

Testing:
- Build succeeds
- All semantic matching tests pass (11/11)
- Manual verification: inferTaxonomy removed from compiled code

🤖 Generated with Claude Code
https://claude.com/claude-code

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add Epic 17 - Portal Data Persistence & Reliability stories

Epic addressing portal data zeroing on refresh. Root causes identified:
- org_id NULL in klaviyo_campaign/flow_metrics (100% of rows)
- Decoupled state via localStorage between layout and pages
- No client-side cache (useState only, no SWR/persist)
- Cache miss + live fetch cooldown/timeout cascade
- API returning 200 OK with empty data instead of errors
- Session not renewed on /api/ routes

7 stories covering DB, Backend, Frontend, and Auth layers.
QA reviewed and approved (PASS).

v1.5.3: Fix banner/header breaking on SPA navigation

Root cause: co-swap was replacing the main site <header> (nav, sticky,
dropdowns) on every navigation. outerHTML replacement destroys all JS
event listeners, causing menus and sticky behavior to break after
the first SPA click and getting worse on back/forward.

Changes:
- Remove nav header selectors (header, #masthead, #site-header, etc.)
  from co-swap — these are identical across pages and must keep JS state
- Add SKIP_IDS set for known navigation/footer element IDs
- Add innerHTML diff check before any co-swap: only replace elements
  whose content actually changed between pages (skips nav = same on all
  pages; swaps page banners = different per page)
- Keep page-specific banner selectors (#page-header, .hero-section, etc.)

commit.message:
Switch titleblocks from raster PDF to vector DXF rendering

Add TitleBlockDxfItem that parses DXF files using ezdxf and renders
all SPLINE/LWPOLYLINE/LINE/CIRCLE/ARC entities as QPainterPaths for
crisp vector output at any zoom level. Fallback chain: DXF → PDF →
programmatic titleblock. Both ANSI B and ANSI D DXF titleblocks load
correctly with ~4800 vector paths each.

fix: 修复自定义套餐天数计算不准确的问题

问题：
- 12个月套餐显示为360天，而不是365天
- 使用 months * 30 计算天数不准确

修复：
- 前端：使用 months * 30.44 计算天数（1个月平均30.44天）
- 后端：使用 Math.Round(months * 30.44) 计算天数
- 12个月 = 365天，6个月 = 183天，更符合实际

示例：
- 1个月：30天
- 3个月：91天
- 6个月：183天
- 12个月：365天

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add caching to single GT proxy to prevent 429 rate limits

Root cause: Vercel IP gets rate-limited by GeckoTerminal (30 req/min).
Homepage batch (7 URLs) + detail page single fetch = too many requests.

- Add per-URL cache with stale-while-revalidate to /api/gt route
  (30s fresh, 5min stale, return stale on errors)
- Add 429 retry (1 attempt, 2s delay) in client fetchWithTimeout
- Return stale cached data on fetch errors as fallback

commit.message:
perf(aam): revert batch concurrency from 10 back to 5

Concurrency=10 caused a 50s→90s regression in step 8. Root cause:
Render instances are resource-constrained — 10 concurrent pipes
overwhelm Farm's DB pool (max 7), saturate network bandwidth to
DCL, and serialize on DCL's sync ingest path. At concurrency=5,
4 waves of ~12s each (48s) outperforms 2 waves of ~45s each (90s).

commit.message:
feat: full-stack audit remediation — security, SEO, error handling, telemetry

Phase 1 (Security):
- SQL injection: blocked SELECT subqueries + multi-statement injection
- Search sanitization: comprehensive regex for PostgREST operators
- Date param bounds: z.coerce 1-365 days max
- Admin error responses sanitized (no db internals leaked)

Phase 2 (Auth):
- Password complexity: 12+ chars, uppercase, lowercase, number, special char
- Timing-safe session validation (no early returns)

Phase 3 (SEO):
- Sitemap ISR revalidation (86400s)
- Tool page schemas preserved (FAQ, breadcrumb, speakable, canonical)

Phase 4 (Error Handling):
- 6 error boundaries (providers, internet, blog, zip, compare, tools)
- 9 loading skeletons with route-specific layouts
- console.error wrapped in dev-only check

Phase 5 (Performance):
- Sitemap revalidation via ISR

Phase 6 (Code Quality):
- Fixed loose `any` types (frontier-fiber, zip, admin error)
- Added apiResponse.ts, sentryContext.ts, truncateDescription.ts utilities

Phase 7 (Telemetry):
- Request ID tracking via x-request-id header in middleware
- Sentry context helper for admin operations

fix(klaviyo): reject non-ASCII API keys to prevent ByteString crash [Story 16.6]

A store had a bullet character (U+2022) in its Klaviyo API key, causing
fetch() to crash with a TypeError on every cron sync attempt. The error
wasted retries and blocked all data for that store permanently.

- Add validateCredentialField() in credentials.service.ts to reject
  non-ASCII keys at save time (meta_access_token excluded — OAuth only)
- Add KlaviyoInvalidKeyError in client.ts for fail-fast on ByteString
  TypeError without wasting retries
- Export KlaviyoInvalidKeyError through the klaviyo barrel
- Add 16 unit + integration tests (credentials) and 3 tests (client)
